sections.paragraph:
γλωσσόκομον, ου, τό (κομέω ‘tend, care for’; H. Gk. for γλωττοκομεῖον, Phryn. 98 Lob.; s. B-D-F §119, 5; Mlt-H. 272; loanw. in rabb.) orig. a ‘case’ for the mouthpiece or reed of a flute, then gener. ‘case, container’ for anything at all (Michel 1001 VIII, 25; 31 [c. 200 b.c.]; pap, e.g., PGrenf I, 14, 3 [II b.c.]; PTebt 414, 21; POxy 521, 12; PGM 13, 1009; and s. Preis.; LXX; Jos., Ant. 6, 11) in NT of a container for money, money-box, purse (Plut., Galba 1060 [16, 2]; PRyl 127, 25 [29 a.d.]; 2 Ch 24:8, 10) J 12:6; τὸ γλωσσόκομον εἶχεν Ἰούδας Judas was in charge of the (common) purse 13:29 (in ref. to Judas’ function as treasurer of the band of disciples).—M-M.

  1. Gk. H. Gk. = Hellenistic Greek

Phryn Phryn , the Atticist, signifying his Eclogae, II a.d.—List 5

Lob. Lob. = CLobeck, s. Phryn.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

loanw. loanw. = loanword

rabb. rabb. = rabbinical (literature etc.)

orig. orig. = original(ly)

gener. gener. = generally

Michel Michel = CM., Recueil d’inscriptions grecques—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

PGrenf I PGrenf I = BGrenfell, An Alexandrian Erotic Fragment, etc.—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

Preis. Preis. = FPreisigke, Wörterbuch der griech. Papyrusurkunden—Lists 4, 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

NT NT = New Testament

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

ref. ref. = reference(s)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 202
